title: Make plus FAB on Hub page open PostTaskModal

requirements.text: Wire the floating action button (FAB) in the bottom-right corner of HubView so that clicking it opens the PostTaskModal for authenticated users who have a profile. If the user is not authenticated or has no profile, trigger the existing auth/profile prompt flow (matching the behaviour already used by TaskCard clicks).

quotes: make the plus button in the right bottom side of the hub page functional so that the user can post a task through it

acceptanceCriteria: Clicking the FAB on HubView opens PostTaskModal for a logged-in user with a profile.
Clicking the FAB when the user is not authenticated shows the existing authentication prompt (AuthPromptBar or equivalent flow).
Clicking the FAB when the user is authenticated but has no profile shows the existing profile-creation prompt flow.
After a task is successfully submitted via the modal, the task list on HubView refreshes to include the new task.
The FAB remains visible and accessible at the bottom-right of the page across all filter/category states.

constraints: Do not modify any files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
Re-use the existing PostTaskModal component — do not create a duplicate modal.
Re-use the existing auth/profile guard logic already present in HubView rather than duplicating it.
nonGoals: Changes to the PostTaskModal form fields or submission logic.
Changes to backend task creation logic.
Any redesign of the FAB button appearance.
